data.body:
## Context

When agents are asked to fix bugs via /dso:fix-bug, two failure modes undermine investigation quality. First, the agent sometimes skips the Skill tool entirely and attempts to fix the bug using its own reasoning, bypassing the structured investigation workflow. Second, even when the skill loads correctly, agents skip hypothesis testing (Step 3) and jump directly to implementation — treating the bug description as confirmed evidence rather than a hypothesis to validate. This leads to fixes that address symptoms rather than root causes, wasted effort on wrong hypotheses, and regressions when the actual root cause is missed.

## Success Criteria

1. All investigation prompts (basic, intermediate, advanced, escalated, cluster) require a structured hypothesis_tests section in the RESULT schema — each entry has four fields: hypothesis (what is being tested), test (the command or check run), observed (what actually happened), and verdict (confirmed or disproved). An investigation result without at least one entry in hypothesis_tests is invalid and triggers escalation to the next investigation tier.
2. The fix-bug orchestrator validates that the investigation result contains at least one hypothesis_tests entry with verdict=confirmed before allowing fix implementation (Step 6) — if all hypotheses are disproved or the section is missing, the orchestrator escalates to the next investigation tier per the existing escalation path (BASIC → INTERMEDIATE → ADVANCED → ESCALATED → User).
3. No code-modifying action (Edit, Write, or fix implementation sub-agent dispatch) occurs before a RED test demonstrates the root cause — the orchestrator enforces RED-before-fix as a hard gate. Mechanical bugs (import errors, lint violations, config syntax) are exempt per the existing Mechanical Fix Path.
4. A UserPromptSubmit hook registered in the plugin's plugin.json detects when the user's message contains /fix-bug or /dso:fix-bug and injects a directive requiring the agent to invoke the Skill tool before any other action. Note: a spike task must first verify that plugin-registered UserPromptSubmit hooks fire reliably (GitHub issue anthropics/claude-code#10225 reported intermittent failures for plugin-level UserPromptSubmit hooks). If plugin-level registration is unreliable, fall back to documenting the hook in project-setup for client .claude/settings.local.json installation.
5. The fix-bug SKILL.md preamble includes a hard gate reinforcing that Steps 1-5 (classify, investigate, hypothesis test, approve, RED test) must complete before any code modification.
6. Unit tests verify the orchestrator hypothesis validation gate: a test with an investigation result missing hypothesis_tests confirms the orchestrator rejects it; a test with all verdict=disproved confirms escalation; a test with at least one verdict=confirmed confirms progression to fix implementation.

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Two-pronged enforcement: (A) Add a mandatory structured hypothesis_tests section to all investigation sub-agent prompts and validate its presence in the orchestrator before allowing fix implementation. (B) Add a UserPromptSubmit hook via plugin.json that detects /fix-bug invocations and injects a directive to use the Skill tool, addressing the skill dispatch reliability problem. A spike task verifies plugin-level UserPromptSubmit reliability before full implementation.

## Supersedes

Bug d22f-da61 (fix-bug skill skips hypothesis testing)
